channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rock icon Jeff Beck's guitars are going on display in London before they are put up for auction
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Some of the most iconic guitars in rock history are going on display in London before they go under the hammer at auction. Auctioneer Christie’s held a preview on Tuesday for the collection of the late Jeff Beck, who died in January 2023 at age 78.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Strong winds forecast for LA threaten wildfire fight
item.description: Additional water tankers and scores of firefighters have arrived in the Los Angeles area ahead of fierce winds that were forecast to return and threaten the progress made so far on two massive infernos that have destroyed thousands of homes and killed at least 24 people.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: NATO announces a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO is launching a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region after a string of incidents in the Baltic that have heightened concerns about possible Russian activities in the region, the alliance's leader said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Firefighters brace for strong winds that could fuel Los Angeles fire growth
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A beefed-up firefighting force was staged around Los Angeles to attack flare-ups or new blazes and anxious residents were prepared for the worst as dangerous winds were expected to build Tuesday, a week after two massive infernos destroyed thousands of homes and killed at least 24
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 153 winners of Nobel and World Food prizes seek new ways to grow food to meet surging global need
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — More than 150 recipients of the Nobel and World Food prizes released an open letter Tuesday calling for a dramatic increase in research and a commitment to new food distribution efforts with a goal of producing more crops and avoiding a global hunger crisis in coming decades.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: Multiracial boom in 2020 census was mostly an illusion, researchers say
item.description: When the 2020 census results were released, they showed a boom in the number of people classified as multiracial in the United States since 2010. Two Princeton sociologists now say that jump was mostly an illusion.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Sheriff calls on wildfire evacuees to be patient amid 'grim' search for bodies
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna asked community members to be patient with local officials, as residents eagerly await permission to return to their homes in southern California, where tens of thousands of acres have been decimated by wildfires in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Colorado apartment complex where armed gang members were seen on video will be closed
item.description: AURORA, Colo. (AP) — A Colorado apartment complex where armed members of a Venezuelan gang were caught on video entering a unit last summer is expected to close in about a month under an emergency court order, city officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires tearing through the Los Angeles area have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 structures in what might be the most expensive conflagrations in the nation's history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: California police arrest multiple drone pilots as firefighters battle infernos
item.description: Los Angeles police arrested three people following drone incidents in wildfire areas as first responders battle infernos raging in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UFC champion booted from Frontier flight after being told he couldn't sit in exit row
item.description: Former UFC lightweight champion Khabib Nurmagomedov was removed from a Frontier Airways flight on Saturday after refusing to state whether he would be able to assist other travelers in the event of an emergency, a spokesperson for Frontier told Nexstar on
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UFC champion booted from Frontier flight after being told he couldn't sit in exit row
item.description: Khabib Nurmagomedov suggested that there may have been “racial” or “national” motivations for booting him from the plane — allegations which Frontier has denied.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Anita Bryant, singer known for opposing gay rights, dies at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant, a former Miss Oklahoma, Grammy-nominated singer and prominent booster of orange juice and other products who became known over the second half of her life for her outspoken opposition to gay rights, has died. She was 84.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump flies US flag at full height before end of 30-day mourning period following Carter's death
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — U.S. flags at President-elect Donald Trump 's private Mar-a-Lago club are back to flying at full height. Flags are supposed to fly at half-staff through the end of January out of respect for former President Jimmy Carter, who died on Dec. 29.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Teacher arrested after threat that shut down NJ school district, police say
item.description: A New Jersey teacher has been arrested after a potential threat to staff and students shut down an entire school district on Monday, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cannon allows for release of Smith report on Trump election interference
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon on Monday allowed the release of the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s efforts to block the peaceful transfer of power after the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lamar Jackson and Josh Allen will face off for a berth in the AFC championship game
item.description: Lamar Jackson vs. Josh Allen won’t decide the NFL MVP next week. The stakes are higher with a spot in the AFC championship game on the line.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 24 dead as fire crews try to corral Los Angeles blazes before winds return this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled Sunday to make further progress against wildfires that have destroyed thousands of homes and killed 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, WGN 9

item.title: Special counsel Jack Smith has resigned after submitting his Trump report, Justice Department says
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith has resigned from the Justice Department after submitting his investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump, an expected move that comes amid legal wrangling over how much of that document can be made public in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, ABC 4

item.title: Winter weather road tips: Staying safe while navigating the snow and ice
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Snowfall brings a whole bundle of headaches for drivers, but there are some steps that can be taken to avoid lingering issues or a potentially dangerous situation.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KRON4

item.title: Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died
item.description: Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died. He was 84.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Disney pledges $15 million in Los Angeles fire aid as more stars learn they've lost their homes
item.description: Disney says it will donate $15 million to respond to the fires and help rebuild. The company announced the donations Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Flying block of snow smashes through Kansas truck driver's windshield
item.description: Clean the snow off your vehicle. It's a warning many northerners have heard before; but one video taken during a recent winter storm illustrates just how dangerous leftover snow can be.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos: Smoke, smoldering homes and flames as wildfires ravage Los Angeles area
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
